name: Fabrication
tagline: A forge-born collective of smiths, machinists, crafters, and toolwrights.
desc: Fabrication Unions build the bones of society — blades, bolts, hinges, and shields. Whether forging a sword, designing a firearm, or crafting mining tools, they value skill, consistency, and measurable results. Every item tells the maker's story.
location: Foundries, machine shops, rail-line forges, or hardened craft halls heavy with smoke, hammering, and the scent of oiled leather.

leaders.name: Belkin Varr
leaders.desc: One-armed master smith, speaks only through apprentices

leaders.name: "Granite" Elga
leaders.desc: Forewoman who drinks molten steel (or claims to)

leaders.name: Nello Skive
leaders.desc: Precision-obsessed artificer with military contracts

leaders.name: Corrick Tom
leaders.desc: Chatty tinker who's secretly the best gunsmith in five cities

goods.name: Reinforced Weapon
goods.desc: +1 to damage and can't be disarmed for 1 day

goods.name: Field Plate Patch
goods.desc: Temporary +10 armor and immunity to shove/push

goods.name: Custom Grip
goods.desc: +1 to weapon checks made by the wielder

goods.name: Gear Harness
goods.desc: Attach tools or weapons for fast switching in combat

goods.name: Rivet Spike
goods.desc: Single-use trap or door wedge

goods.name: Foldaway Shield
goods.desc: Compact, deployable, gives +5 armor once per combat

merchantFeatures.name: Workbench Access
merchantFeatures.desc: You may craft or modify a mundane weapon or tool once per rest

merchantFeatures.name: Blueprint Recall
merchantFeatures.desc: Once per scene, declare that you "built that already" and produce a small, simple device

merchantFeatures.name: Union Tag
merchantFeatures.desc: Your gear is marked with certified maker's stamps — sell for +25% in towns with a Fabrication presence

merchantFeatures.name: Scrap Reclaim
merchantFeatures.desc: Break down 1 item per town to recover usable materials worth up to 25gp

infoAccess.name: Forge Order
infoAccess.desc: Learn what items a local faction is ordering in bulk

infoAccess.name: Break Pattern
infoAccess.desc: Identify a common flaw in a local enemy's gear or defenses

infoAccess.name: Tool Route
infoAccess.desc: Discover where material shipments or work crews are operating

name: Harvest
tagline: A collective of farmers, miners, foragers, and resource gatherers.
desc: Harvest Unions are the lifeblood of regional supply, trading sweat for sustenance. They manage fields, forests, quarries, and fisheries — bringing raw goods to market and controlling who gets to eat, build, or burn. Where the Martial Unions guard the cities, the Harvest Unions keep the wilds habitable.
location: Sawmills, smokehouses, farm co-ops, quarry lodges, or smoke-stained bunkhouses lined with old tools and fresh dirt.

leaders.name: Cobb Verren
leaders.desc: One-eyed cattle boss with a boot for every season

leaders.name: Gretta Stonehook
leaders.desc: Former miner, face lined with soot and smile-wrinkles

leaders.name: Marl Patch
leaders.desc: Nomadic herbalist who speaks in local dialects

leaders.name: Ramel Brine
leaders.desc: Fisherman who never steps far from the riverbank

goods.name: Crate of Raw Goods
goods.desc: 1 unit of tradeable material (grain, ore, lumber, etc.)

goods.name: Forager's Bundle
goods.desc: Rare herbs, pelts, or bones — sells high or aids crafting

goods.name: Extraction Permit
goods.desc: Legal access to restricted land for gathering

goods.name: Pack Beasts
goods.desc: Temporary increase to travel carry capacity or speed

goods.name: Field Tools
goods.desc: Reduces fatigue for physical labor or speeds up harvesting

goods.name: Smuggled Harvest
goods.desc: Illegal goods pulled from contested or cursed land

merchantFeatures.name: Supply Runner
merchantFeatures.desc: You can source 1 unit of basic material per town

merchantFeatures.name: Beast Bond
merchantFeatures.desc: You gain a work animal (mule, ox, giant beetle) that can carry, defend, or assist

merchantFeatures.name: Field Broker
merchantFeatures.desc: Sell gathered resources at +25% value in towns with a Harvest presence

merchantFeatures.name: Claim Flag
merchantFeatures.desc: Mark a wild zone as claimed by your Union — local poachers or rivals may face penalties or resistance

infoAccess.name: Resource Spot
infoAccess.desc: Discover a new viable harvest site within your current region

infoAccess.name: Territory Dispute
infoAccess.desc: Learn if another Union is interfering or illegally extracting nearby

infoAccess.name: Weather Sign
infoAccess.desc: Gain insight into incoming natural shifts (blight, drought, migration, storm)

name: Refinement
tagline: A league of brewers, alchemists, augmenters, and field engineers.
desc: Refinement Unions solve problems through mixtures, tools, and tweaks. Whether concocting a stimulant, modifying a weapon, or reinforcing armor on the go, their methods are repeatable, mechanical, and strictly non-mystical.
location: Industrial labs, tonic dens, boiler sheds, or cluttered workshops heavy with fumes, bubbling glass, and soot-slick aprons.

leaders.name: Tanna Greaves
leaders.desc: Alchemist with ink-stained sleeves and burned eyebrows

leaders.name: Eorl Wix
leaders.desc: Gearcrafter who speaks in clicks and diagrams

leaders.name: Doc Bramble
leaders.desc: Brewery engineer, always holding something bubbling

leaders.name: Sint Vatch
leaders.desc: Modder-for-hire with a cough and too many tools

goods.name: Reinforcing Draft
goods.desc: Temporarily grants +25 armor for 1 scene

goods.name: Potent Stimulant
goods.desc: Target gains +1d4 to all checks for 10 minutes, then 1 Wound

goods.name: Weapon Polish Kit
goods.desc: Adds +1 to a weapon's damage rolls until rest

goods.name: Alchemical Patch
goods.desc: Removes 1 Condition or stabilizes a Wounded ally

goods.name: Smokecharge Pellet
goods.desc: Creates a 10ft smoke cloud, obscures vision

goods.name: Stability Syringe
goods.desc: Once per session, ignore the next negative condition effect

merchantFeatures.name: Portable Kit
merchantFeatures.desc: Create an Upgrade Potion over a long rest that lasts for 1 week and can be applied to a weapon to increase its Check and Damage by +1 (once per week per weapon)

merchantFeatures.name: Field Tinkering
merchantFeatures.desc: Once per day, modify a piece of gear for +3 to checks until a Long Rest

merchantFeatures.name: Formula Access
merchantFeatures.desc: Brew any known alchemical item with one hour and proper materials

merchantFeatures.name: No-Frag Rule
merchantFeatures.desc: Identify faulty or sabotaged items, traps, or brews on sight

infoAccess.name: Tonic Trend
infoAccess.desc: Learn which brews, drugs, or enhancements are being widely used or banned

infoAccess.name: Upgrade Request
infoAccess.desc: Discover if any NPCs or factions are commissioning specific gear

infoAccess.name: Brew Scarcity
infoAccess.desc: Learn which materials are in short supply for crafting in this region

name: Commerce
tagline: A cartel of traders, brokers, auctioneers, and financial liaisons.
desc: Commerce Unions do not sell things — they sell access. They regulate pricing, control trade routes, and determine who is licensed to profit. Backroom deals, storefront licenses, and bulk contracts give them subtle dominance over every coin spent.
location: Trade halls, auction floors, Unionmarts, counting houses, or tiled offices packed with ledgers, trade permits, and tense conversations.

leaders.name: Jentha Fale
leaders.desc: Runs silent auctions with a crooked grin and a silver hammer

leaders.name: Mister Scrip
leaders.desc: Never seen touching money, always knows who owes what

leaders.name: Mae Varn
leaders.desc: Former merchant-ship captain, known for brutal efficiency

leaders.name: Burrim Quill
leaders.desc: Middle-aged pen-pusher who somehow owns half the market

goods.name: Temporary Stall Permit
goods.desc: Grants the right to sell in any market for 24 hours

goods.name: Margin Contract
goods.desc: Gain +25% profit on a single transaction

goods.name: Buyout Clause
goods.desc: Remove a low-tier competitor from the market temporarily

goods.name: Liquid Credit Slip
goods.desc: Spend this in place of 50gp, redeemable post-session

goods.name: Escrow Bond
goods.desc: Secure a deal between two parties without full trust

goods.name: Price Fix Ledger
goods.desc: Temporarily lock the value of one item at a chosen rate

merchantFeatures.name: Market Leverage
merchantFeatures.desc: Gain +1d4 to any Influence or Barter check involving trade

merchantFeatures.name: Preferred Vendor
merchantFeatures.desc: You may buy one item per town at 25% off base value

merchantFeatures.name: License Holder
merchantFeatures.desc: You may legally run a stall, shop, or brokerage in any town with a Commerce presence

merchantFeatures.name: Fast Exchange
merchantFeatures.desc: Once per town, swap any item you own for one of equal value (narrator approved)

infoAccess.name: Market Pulse
infoAccess.desc: Learn what item or good is in demand (sells high)

infoAccess.name: Credit Risk
infoAccess.desc: Discover whether a named NPC, shop, or faction is in debt

infoAccess.name: Trade Movement
infoAccess.desc: See where large shipments are headed or delayed

name: Transport
tagline: A union of pilots, freight runners, railmasters, and route controllers.
desc: Transport Unions control how people and goods move. They secure roads, maintain schedules, and determine who gets there first — or at all. Whether by zeppelin, mule, train, or boot, if it moves through civilized space, they likely signed off on it.
location: Freight yards, airship docks, cable car stations, rail depots, or dispatch towers, often with scattered crates, loudspeakers, and logbooks stacked to the ceiling.

leaders.name: Lockjaw Dren
leaders.desc: Ex-sky captain with a whistle always between her teeth

leaders.name: Cyrus Wain
leaders.desc: Chain-smoking route clerk with perfect recall

leaders.name: Siggin Toll
leaders.desc: Rail veteran who measures everything in time lost or gained

leaders.name: Eido Sash
leaders.desc: Wears goggles even indoors, rarely speaks, always knows where to go

goods.name: Transit Voucher
goods.desc: Free passage for your group on the next available route

goods.name: Courier Tag
goods.desc: Grants priority delivery of a letter, item, or sealed crate

goods.name: Detour Chart
goods.desc: Use this to avoid a known blockade, toll, or hazard

goods.name: Shift Permit
goods.desc: Reroutes a standard transit route in your favor for one day

goods.name: Emergency Recall Key
goods.desc: Summons a ride or transport to your location once

goods.name: Hauler's Ledger
goods.desc: Trade contacts or regional route info stored in shorthand

merchantFeatures.name: Ticket Line
merchantFeatures.desc: You can always secure basic travel for yourself and one ally, even in crisis

merchantFeatures.name: Cargo Claim
merchantFeatures.desc: Once per region, reroute a crate or shipment to your location

merchantFeatures.name: Road Eyes
merchantFeatures.desc: +1 to Navigation checks or travel planning

merchantFeatures.name: Waypost Access
merchantFeatures.desc: You know the location of hidden, protected Union shelters along major routes

infoAccess.name: Route Forecast
infoAccess.desc: Learn the fastest or safest path to a location

infoAccess.name: Disruption Log
infoAccess.desc: See if any roads, tracks, or ports are under threat or delay

infoAccess.name: Manifest Glimpse
infoAccess.desc: Learn who or what recently passed through a given transit point

name: Martial
tagline: A disciplined network of professional enforcers, bounty hunters, and hired blades.
desc: Martial Unions keep cities clean, protect shipments, and ensure contracts are honored — sometimes with bullets, sometimes with bodies. They operate on rigid structure, ironclad deals, and a public image of strength. Their presence is usually loud, uniformed, and undeniable.
location: Fortified compounds, contract boards, city guard annexes, or private security towers staffed by trench-coated veterans, steel-eyed clerks, and the occasional retired war mage.

leaders.name: Captain Derrow
leaders.desc: Straight-backed old vet, loyal to a fault

leaders.name: "Mad" Brisa Tull
leaders.desc: Whiskey-drinking bounty hunter turned organizer

leaders.name: The Sergeant
leaders.desc: Anonymous, face always covered, speaks through others

leaders.name: Benny Hooks
leaders.desc: Ex-mafia enforcer legitimized by a badge

goods.name: Soldier (PL10)
goods.desc: Sword, sidearm, heavy armor

goods.name: Spellsword (PL8)
goods.desc: Sword, basic origin, light armor

goods.name: Brute (PL7)
goods.desc: Greatsword, +100 armor, shock gloves

goods.name: Two Crows (PL6)
goods.desc: Twin saboteurs, knives, smoke bombs

goods.name: Dogpack (PL6)
goods.desc: Three trained hounds; tracking and melee

goods.name: Rifleman (PL5)
goods.desc: Long rifle, trick shots, smoke bombs

merchantFeatures.name: Hireling
merchantFeatures.desc: Gain a permanent PL5 mercenary

merchantFeatures.name: Gearwork
merchantFeatures.desc: +1 to weapon and armor checks and damage

merchantFeatures.name: Command Chain
merchantFeatures.desc: Once per day, issue a verbal command; one ally gains +1d4 to a combat roll

merchantFeatures.name: Shield Contract
merchantFeatures.desc: Your group is flagged as under protection; guards give +1 Favor in towns with Martial presence

infoAccess.name: Active Bounty
infoAccess.desc: Get the name, last known location, and reward for a regional target

infoAccess.name: Union Watchlist
infoAccess.desc: Learn if any local NPCs or factions are flagged as hostile, criminal, or under surveillance

infoAccess.name: Safe Route
infoAccess.desc: Get a guarded or conflict-free travel path to the next settlement or objective

name: Culture
tagline: A network of performers, publishers, stylemakers, and behind-the-scenes handlers.
desc: Culture Unions shape the public voice — through stage, page, broadcast, and spectacle. Whether they run opera houses, print tabloid gossip, or choreograph street protests, their work moves people with emotion, color, and spin.
location: Theaters, nightclubs, printshops, cabarets, and radio stations; usually surrounded by lights, noise, and cigarette haze.

leaders.name: Felix Bloom
leaders.desc: Veteran playwright turned kingmaker with a velvet scarf

leaders.name: Dame Letta
leaders.desc: Opera singer, now producer, who talks like she's still on stage

leaders.name: Mr. Paddock
leaders.desc: Silent film director known for explosive scandals

leaders.name: Minnie Crim
leaders.desc: Gossip queen and editor of the Sablepier Echo

goods.name: Flash Poster Run
goods.desc: Quickly spreads a message, slogan, or face through a district

goods.name: Performance Permit
goods.desc: Grants the right to speak, sing, or protest in a public space

goods.name: Blacklist Ledger
goods.desc: Contains the names of those recently canceled or slandered

goods.name: Radio Segment
goods.desc: Brief broadcast used to influence morale or perception in a town

goods.name: Agent Contract
goods.desc: Grants temporary access to a loyal stagehand, hype agent, or journalist

goods.name: Costuming Kit
goods.desc: Grants +1d4 to a disguise, impersonation, or on-stage deception

merchantFeatures.name: Spotlight
merchantFeatures.desc: Gain +1 Favor in any scene where you perform or make a public display

merchantFeatures.name: Whispersheet
merchantFeatures.desc: Once per day, seed a rumor or story into local news — true or false

merchantFeatures.name: Access Pass
merchantFeatures.desc: Always allowed into entertainment venues, lounges, and social hubs

merchantFeatures.name: Stagehand
merchantFeatures.desc: You gain a personal assistant NPC who specializes in distraction or flare

infoAccess.name: Trending Topic
infoAccess.desc: Learn what's capturing local attention or stirring unrest

infoAccess.name: Public Sentiment
infoAccess.desc: Gain insight into a location's attitude toward a group or event

infoAccess.name: Media Leverage
infoAccess.desc: Learn if any public figures are vulnerable to exposure or influence

name: Politics
tagline: A tangled web of lawmakers, administrators, clerks, and power brokers.
desc: Politics Unions do not hold swords — they hold signatures, sealed files, and rubber stamps. Their strength lies in permits, legal delays, shifting ordinances, and the subtle rerouting of authority. They do not shout — they file.
location: Courthouses, permit offices, administrative towers, law libraries, or smoky backrooms where policy is bought more than it is made.

leaders.name: Magistrate Kell
leaders.desc: Always calm, never seen without gloves, reputation spotless

leaders.name: Darnet Quill
leaders.desc: Bureaucrat turned puppeteer, "just a clerk" with 40 strings pulled

leaders.name: Silba Trask
leaders.desc: Young reformer with dangerous charisma and old enemies

leaders.name: "The Whisperer"
leaders.desc: Nobody's seen them — just their directives, always stamped correctly

goods.name: Temporary Permit
goods.desc: Grants access to a restricted area or function for 24 hours

goods.name: Sealed Order
goods.desc: Deliver this to reroute guards, pause an operation, or delay action

goods.name: Arbitration Letter
goods.desc: Used to resolve (or suppress) disputes between Unions or factions

goods.name: Official Seal
goods.desc: When shown, gives +1d4 to Influence or legal interaction rolls

goods.name: Denial Notice
goods.desc: Blocks a rival's action, project, or public gathering for a day

goods.name: Amendment Copy
goods.desc: Allows rewriting or voiding a minor contract or law (with roll)

merchantFeatures.name: Stamp Access
merchantFeatures.desc: You may request one temporary permit per town for personal or party use

merchantFeatures.name: Soft Favor
merchantFeatures.desc: Gain +1 Influence with bureaucrats, city guards, or legal factions

merchantFeatures.name: Delay Tactic
merchantFeatures.desc: Once per day, stall an enemy or official action by one scene

merchantFeatures.name: Board Seat
merchantFeatures.desc: You gain a named, if ceremonial, position — your votes shape local decisions

infoAccess.name: Active Motion
infoAccess.desc: Learn what new law or Union policy is about to pass

infoAccess.name: Leverage File
infoAccess.desc: Discover legal pressure points on a faction or leader

infoAccess.name: Permit Record
infoAccess.desc: See who has recent permits for movement, meetings, or rallies

name: Mysticism
tagline: A formal network of artificers, wardwrights, relic brokers, and sanctioned casters.
desc: Mysticism Unions regulate magic like electricity — powerful, dangerous, and taxable. They create wards, examine artifacts, and sell arcane access to those who can afford it. Equal parts esoteric service and magical licensing board, they do not tolerate freelance sorcery.
location: Marble towers, sigil-inscribed halls, warded vaults, underground sanctums, or public arcana counters with locked backrooms.

leaders.name: Varn Lemein
leaders.desc: Wears gloves over gloves, speaks only when properly addressed

leaders.name: Ariah of the Folded Eye
leaders.desc: Knows too much, trusts nobody, reads auras like faces

leaders.name: Treve Magthorn
leaders.desc: Sells relics with both charm and vague threats

leaders.name: High Ritualist Ink
leaders.desc: Covered in glowing tattoos that map ley-lines

goods.name: Mana Sigil
goods.desc: Charges a spell or item once, bypassing its normal cost or limit

goods.name: Tether Stone
goods.desc: Anchors a person, object, or spirit to its current plane

goods.name: Ward Kit
goods.desc: Temporary glyphs protect a room or entrance from magical intrusion

goods.name: True Ink
goods.desc: Used to sign, seal, or alter magical contracts

goods.name: Glimpse Orb
goods.desc: Shows a short magical memory from a creature or item

goods.name: Curse Tag
goods.desc: Applies a minor affliction or hex to a target (must pass roll to resist)

merchantFeatures.name: Arcane Tag
merchantFeatures.desc: You may mark one item as sanctioned — it cannot be legally confiscated

merchantFeatures.name: Ley Access
merchantFeatures.desc: You can tap into a known ley-line or mana source once per region

merchantFeatures.name: Sigil Trained
merchantFeatures.desc: Gain +1 to checks involving magical tools or relics

merchantFeatures.name: Vault Key
merchantFeatures.desc: Once per town, you may access Union storage to retrieve a tagged item, scroll, or relic worth up to 100gp

infoAccess.name: Item Report
infoAccess.desc: Learn the origin, last use, and danger level of a magical object

infoAccess.name: Spell Echo
infoAccess.desc: Gain a ghost image of the last spell cast in an area

infoAccess.name: Warrant List
infoAccess.desc: See who in the region is licensed, restricted, or forbidden from casting

name: Scholarship
tagline: An assembly of researchers, professors, archivists, and academic bureaucrats.
desc: Scholarship Unions collect, preserve, and guard the written and spoken truths of the world. They maintain libraries, universities, observatories, and deep vaults of forbidden knowledge. Power is found in precision, precedent, and publishing first.
location: Lecture halls, dusty archives, scriptoriums, printing houses, or quiet offices filled with filing cabinets, chalkboards, and stained mugs of tea.

leaders.name: Headmaster Kilner
leaders.desc: Elegant, ancient, and five steps ahead of any debate

leaders.name: Gresha Fen
leaders.desc: Researcher who knows everything except when to stop talking

leaders.name: Dean of Ashes
leaders.desc: Runs the restricted archives and never once smiled

leaders.name: Henrick Blot
leaders.desc: Nervous editor with keys to a hundred sealed manuscripts

goods.name: Verified Ledger
goods.desc: A sourcebook that grants +1 to research-related checks

goods.name: Field Notes
goods.desc: Excerpted info that reveals the weakness or history of a threat

goods.name: Contradiction File
goods.desc: Discredits a known claim or official narrative in the region

goods.name: Draft Publication
goods.desc: Grants temporary prestige (+1 Influence in intellectual circles)

goods.name: Referenced Map
goods.desc: Reveals a previously unknown location or access point

goods.name: Scholar's Favor
goods.desc: Trade this for help from an academic NPC, such as an assistant, translator, or contact

merchantFeatures.name: Peer Review
merchantFeatures.desc: Once per region, submit a paper and gain a +1 to Influence or Research checks for the next 24 hours

merchantFeatures.name: Institutional Trust
merchantFeatures.desc: You may access restricted records or archives in scholarly towns

merchantFeatures.name: Footnote Pull
merchantFeatures.desc: You can ask one question per town and receive a vague but factual answer from local lorekeepers

merchantFeatures.name: Intellect Hound
merchantFeatures.desc: As an off-action once per day, double an intellect check

infoAccess.name: Authoritative Source
infoAccess.desc: Gain insight or confirmation on a specific piece of lore or mystery

infoAccess.name: Obscured Record
infoAccess.desc: Uncover a suppressed, edited, or forgotten historical fact

infoAccess.name: Field Update
infoAccess.desc: Learn what current research projects, expeditions, or academic disputes are active in the region

name: Stewardship
tagline: A coalition of foremen, civil engineers, sanitation directors, and land stewards.
desc: Stewardship Unions ensure the cities don't fall apart at the seams. From managing irrigation canals and sewer lines to rationing coal and rerouting food trucks, they handle the unseen rhythms of civilization. Quietly vital, and they are quick to remind you of it.
location: Utility stations, old city maintenance yards, transit hubs, grain depots, or repurposed town halls filled with clipboards and coal smoke.

leaders.name: Gilda Cask
leaders.desc: Hard-nosed forewoman who carries a steam wrench like a badge

leaders.name: Rameel Moss
leaders.desc: Environmental planner who speaks in long-winded metaphors

leaders.name: Red Eir
leaders.desc: Local legend who remembers every pipeline and street by heart

leaders.name: Inspector Knox
leaders.desc: Pencil-mustached bureaucrat, perfectly clean gloves

goods.name: Emergency Ration Vouchers
goods.desc: Gain access to food, water, or energy for your group

goods.name: Map of City Lines
goods.desc: Learn a shortcut, tunnel, or bypass route (ignores obstacles once)

goods.name: Work Crew Favor
goods.desc: Get temporary laborers for repairs, barricades, or hauling

goods.name: Inspection Permit
goods.desc: Grants legal access to restricted zones for 1 day

goods.name: Portable Generator
goods.desc: Lights a scene, powers a device, or delays collapse

goods.name: Water Purity Kit
goods.desc: Neutralizes a poison, toxin, or environmental hazard in a small area

merchantFeatures.name: Civic Trust
merchantFeatures.desc: +1 Influence with towns, utilities, and rural leaders

merchantFeatures.name: Repair Access
merchantFeatures.desc: You can reroute, stabilize, or bypass infrastructure once per session

merchantFeatures.name: Supply Chain Tag
merchantFeatures.desc: You may request basic goods (food, materials) at no cost once per town

merchantFeatures.name: Outpost License
merchantFeatures.desc: You may designate one player-owned building as an official service point (grants +1 Favor with locals)

infoAccess.name: Resource Strain
infoAccess.desc: Learn which settlements or neighborhoods are running low on key supplies

infoAccess.name: Infrastructure Risk
infoAccess.desc: Get early warnings on upcoming collapses, floods, or shortages

infoAccess.name: Zone Report
infoAccess.desc: Determine which factions have logistical control over a target district

name: Subversion
tagline: A network of smugglers, fixers, counterfeiters, and blackmailers.
desc: Subversion Unions thrive in shadows, turning secrets into power and crime into currency. Their halls are hidden speakeasies, private basements, or front businesses — places where nothing is off-limits and everything has a price.
location: Backroom parlors, boarded-up offices, smoke-filled bars, and underground lounges thick with hush money and whispered deals.

leaders.name: Tobble Soot
leaders.desc: Chain-smoking goblin fence with dirt on everyone

leaders.name: Lyn Virand
leaders.desc: Cold-eyed fixer who never speaks above a whisper

leaders.name: Nails
leaders.desc: Thug-turned-facilitator with a gold tooth and gold ledger

leaders.name: "Cousin" Elle
leaders.desc: Socialite with knives in her purse and blackmail in her smile

goods.name: Forged Credentials
goods.desc: Bypass guard checks or access locked zones

goods.name: Blackmail File
goods.desc: Grants +1d4 Influence when negotiating with a named NPC

goods.name: Smuggler's Route Map
goods.desc: Avoids detection during illegal travel or transport

goods.name: Contraband Vials
goods.desc: Toxic, addictive, or mind-altering; causes chaos or control

goods.name: Disguise Package
goods.desc: Includes voice cover, papers, outfit; grants temporary false identity

goods.name: Listener's Coin
goods.desc: Once per day, lets you eavesdrop on a nearby scene as if present

merchantFeatures.name: Hidden Pockets
merchantFeatures.desc: Once per day, reveal a small illegal item you already had

merchantFeatures.name: Blacklist Pull
merchantFeatures.desc: Learn the true reputation of any NPC or faction

merchantFeatures.name: False Badge
merchantFeatures.desc: You may impersonate a Union, office, or organization without immediate suspicion

merchantFeatures.name: Deniable Deal
merchantFeatures.desc: If you are caught doing something illegal, pin it on a paper trail and roll Influence to walk

infoAccess.name: Surveillance Log
infoAccess.desc: Learn who is watching your group or your contacts

infoAccess.name: Corruption List
infoAccess.desc: See who is being paid off in town, including guards, officials, or merchants

infoAccess.name: Breach Warning
infoAccess.desc: Discover where Martial Union plans to move or strike next
